rules.source_of_truth: This file is the source of truth for planned harness and product work.

features.title: Initialize minimal harness structure
features.user_visible_behavior: A fresh agent session can identify the repository purpose, startup command, verification command, current progress, and next action from repo files.

verification.command: ./scripts/verify-harness.sh
steps: Confirm required harness files exist.
Confirm init.sh is executable.
Confirm feature_list.json contains required fields.
Confirm AGENTS.md contains startup workflow, required artifacts, definition of done, and end-of-session instructions.
Confirm AGENTS.md links to startup and harness docs.

features.evidence: ./init.sh passed on 2026-05-25; scripts/verify-harness.sh reported: Harness verification passed. AGENTS.md hardened with end-of-session instructions on 2026-05-25.
features.notes: Created before the product stack is selected; later tightened against the provided AGENTS.md template.

features.title: Choose project direction and initial local learning stack
features.user_visible_behavior: A fresh agent session can identify that this project is a Mattermost ChatOps bot for authenticating users to a Kubernetes cluster-management backend, and can see the first local learning path.

verification.command: ./init.sh
steps: Record the chosen project goal in README.md and AGENTS.md.
Record the K3s-first and Mattermost-local learning path.
Record open decisions for bot runtime and backend API contract.
Keep host-level setup commands explicit instead of auto-installing from init.sh.
Run the updated init.sh successfully.

features.evidence: Project direction recorded on 2026-05-26 in README.md, AGENTS.md, docs/project-direction.md, docs/local-environment.md, DECISIONS.md, and PROGRESS.md. ./init.sh passed on 2026-05-26.
features.notes: The backend API and bot implementation runtime remain intentionally undecided; they are tracked as later work.

features.title: Verify local K3s access
features.user_visible_behavior: A developer can confirm local Kubernetes access with K3s and see recorded evidence of node, namespace, and cluster-info checks.

verification.command: Historical manual K3s verification plus ./init.sh
steps: Confirm the local K3s installation or access method.
Run kubectl or sudo k3s kubectl node and namespace checks.
Record exact commands and summarized output in PROGRESS.md.
Update docs/local-environment.md with any machine-specific setup notes.
Run ./init.sh successfully.

features.evidence: On 2026-05-26, installed k3d 5.8.3 with Homebrew and created local cluster csit-lab with one server and one agent. The temporary K3s learning verifier passed for kubectl context k3d-csit-lab. Evidence: kubectl cluster-info reached the control plane; kubectl get nodes -o wide showed k3d-csit-lab-server-0 and k3d-csit-lab-agent-0 Ready on K3s v1.33.6+k3s1; kubectl get namespaces showed default, kube-node-lease, kube-public, and kube-system Active.
features.notes: Local macOS path used Docker Desktop plus k3d instead of a host-level K3s service. K3s was a completed learning stage; the active production bot harness no longer includes a K3s readiness script.

features.title: Deploy first K3s learning workload
features.user_visible_behavior: A developer can deploy, inspect, log, and clean up a simple workload in a local K3s namespace.

verification.command: Manual workload verification plus ./init.sh
steps: Create a learning namespace.
Deploy a simple workload.
Expose or inspect the workload.
Collect kubectl get, describe, and logs evidence.
Clean up the learning namespace.
Record evidence in PROGRESS.md.
Run ./init.sh successfully.

features.evidence: On 2026-05-26, used kubectl context k3d-csit-lab to create namespace csit-lab, deploy hello-nginx from nginx:stable, expose it on port 80, and verify rollout. Evidence: rollout status reported deployment "hello-nginx" successfully rolled out; kubectl get all --namespace csit-lab -o wide showed pod 1/1 Running, service hello-nginx ClusterIP on 80/TCP, deployment 1/1 available, and matching ReplicaSet 1/1 ready; kubectl logs --namespace csit-lab deploy/hello-nginx showed nginx startup completed. Cleanup evidence: kubectl delete namespace csit-lab completed, and kubectl get namespace csit-lab returned NotFound.
features.notes: Depends on L01. The learning namespace was intentionally removed after verification.

features.title: Set up local Mattermost
features.user_visible_behavior: A developer can start local Mattermost, create a team and channel, and identify the local URL for integration testing.

verification.command: Manual Mattermost verification plus ./init.sh
steps: Choose the local Mattermost deployment path.
Start Mattermost locally.
Create or confirm a local team and channel.
Record URL, deployment command, and cleanup command.
Run ./init.sh successfully.

features.evidence: On 2026-05-26, selected the official Mattermost Docker Compose deployment without NGINX for first local familiarisation after checking current Mattermost docs and after user clarified that the setup should resemble production more closely than the preview image. Cloned https://github.com/mattermost/docker to /private/tmp/mattermost-docker-csit, configured .env with DOMAIN=localhost and MM_SERVICESETTINGS_SITEURL=http://localhost:8065, created the Mattermost volume directories, and added a local Apple Silicon override with platform: linux/amd64 because mattermost/mattermost-enterprise-edition:11.7.0 did not publish a linux/arm64/v8 manifest. Docker Compose started Postgres and Mattermost; docker compose ps showed mattermost-docker-csit-mattermost-1 Up and healthy on 0.0.0.0:8065->8065/tcp and postgres Up. curl http://localhost:8065/api/v4/system/ping returned HTTP/1.1 200 OK with JSON status OK and Mattermost version 11.7.0. ./scripts/verify-mattermost.sh passed with host-local access. Created local admin username csit-admin, team csit-lab with id bfnj9akc4fytxp4gwbn9ak5cer, and channel chatops-lab with id ez1crw7et3fztdsxywargec1sr.
features.notes: Depends on L01 or L02 only if Mattermost will be deployed into K3s; otherwise it can be done with Docker independently. Current chosen path is the official mattermost/docker checkout with docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.without-nginx.yml up -d. On Apple Silicon, include a local override file that sets services.mattermost.platform to linux/amd64. The current checkout and local data live under /private/tmp/mattermost-docker-csit.

features.title: Explore Mattermost bot integration options
features.user_visible_behavior: The repository documents the recommended first integration style for the ChatOps bot and why it fits the authentication workflow.

verification.command: Manual review plus ./init.sh
steps: Review slash commands, webhooks, bot accounts, plugins, and apps.
Record tradeoffs for authentication and local development.
Choose the first prototype integration style.
Record the decision in DECISIONS.md.
Run ./init.sh successfully.

features.evidence: On 2026-05-26, reviewed current Mattermost integration docs for slash commands, incoming webhooks, outgoing webhooks, bot accounts, REST API use, plugins, and the deprecated Apps framework. Added docs/bot-integrations.md with tradeoffs and security notes. Later corrected the first prototype decision to use a dedicated Mattermost bot account named csit because the intended user experience is talking to a stable bot identity, not invoking a slash command from any chat. Confirmed local Mattermost had zero custom slash commands before the bot-account implementation pivot.
features.notes: Backend API contract can remain mocked or pending during this feature. First prototype bot account should be @acorn-ops-bot.

features.title: Choose and scaffold bot implementation runtime
features.user_visible_behavior: The repository contains the first bot skeleton with documented install, test, lint, build, and local run commands.

verification.command: ./init.sh
steps: Choose the bot runtime and framework.
Record versions and rationale.
Scaffold minimal bot source code.
Add install, test, lint, build, and local run commands.
Run ./init.sh successfully.

features.evidence: On 2026-05-26, selected Node.js ECMAScript modules with built-in Node.js runtime APIs and node:test. Added package scripts for install via npm install, test via npm test, lint via npm run lint, build via npm run build, local run via npm start, and full bot verification via npm run verify:bot. Scaffolded src/bot command/message handling, tests, scripts/verify-bot.sh, and docs/bot-runtime.md. npm run verify:bot passed, and ./init.sh passed after adding bot verification to startup.
features.notes: Depends on L03 and L04. The initial scaffold used placeholder responses; B04 and later features replace them with the current AcornOps external integration contract.

features.title: Wire local Mattermost bot account conversation
features.user_visible_behavior: A developer can run the bot locally, talk to the @acorn-ops-bot Mattermost bot account, and verify an end-to-end response in the local Mattermost team.

verification.command: Manual Mattermost bot-account verification plus ./init.sh
steps: Confirm no leftover local /csit custom slash command exists.
Create or confirm the local Mattermost bot account named acorn-ops-bot.
Start the local bot process with a bot access token stored outside committed files.
Send a direct message or mention to @acorn-ops-bot from a non-bot Mattermost user.
Record the bot response evidence.
Run ./init.sh successfully.

features.evidence: On 2026-05-26, confirmed local Mattermost had zero custom slash commands before implementation, created local bot account csit with id 6bcr1d8zxpraxnz77skinxwtoa, generated a bot token outside committed files, and created local test user csit-alice with id rcnutpf7fff4mjthsd1gck5p1y. Replaced the slash-command HTTP receiver with a bot-account process that authenticates to Mattermost, opens the WebSocket event stream, responds to direct messages and @csit mentions, ignores self-authored posts, and posts replies through the Mattermost REST API. npm run verify:bot passed with 12 tests. Manual end-to-end verification passed in direct-message channel fqxfhfozojystmibobinn8p94w: csit-alice sent post dof79es14pyy7fnyifteyieiow with message status; bot reply o6tjjqjohpg6ikkbrqtxhmx34c was authored by csit and contained CSIT status, Mattermost user @csit-alice (rcnutpf7fff4mjthsd1gck5p1y), Backend authentication: not connected, and Cluster access: not loaded. Follow-up verification changed bot replies to normal main-timeline messages: csit-alice sent post n6g8tkb9ypdoiewe3yd87mjqae with message status; bot reply 4pm7b7i43jyxtgj6g19fs15oby had root_id set to an empty string. Final ./init.sh passed.
features.notes: Depends on B01 and L03. The local bot token must stay outside committed files. Bot replies intentionally omit root_id so Mattermost renders them as normal channel or direct-message timeline posts, not threaded replies. The official bot username is now acorn-ops-bot; older evidence used the previous local csit account. Backend login is now tracked in B03 and later auth work.

features.title: Wire first local AcornOps login command
features.user_visible_behavior: A developer can run the bot locally, direct-message @acorn-ops-bot with login, and have the bot call the local AcornOps control-plane API instead of returning a placeholder.

verification.command: ./init.sh
steps: Add an AcornOps control-plane client for the first local login API call.
Use direct-message login only; do not call login from shared channel mentions.
Store the returned AcornOps session in memory by Mattermost user id.
Update status to report the stored AcornOps login state.
Add tests for request shape, cookie capture, direct-message login, channel guard, and runner wiring.
Run ./init.sh successfully.

features.evidence: On 2026-06-04, explored /Users/ryangoh/Desktop/Development/acornops and confirmed the AcornOps control-plane API lives in /Users/ryangoh/Desktop/Development/acornops/control-plane. Added src/bot/acornops-client.js for POST /api/v1/auth/dev-login, src/bot/auth-store.js for in-memory Mattermost-user session storage, and wired direct-message login through the runner and message handler. Added tests for deterministic Mattermost dev-login email, dev-login request body, session-cookie capture, direct-message-only login behavior, status reporting after login, and runner-level login wiring. ./init.sh passed with 18 tests on 2026-06-04. Live AcornOps backend smoke passed against http://localhost:8081: /health returned ok, the CSIT AcornOps client received mode dev with a session cookie for mattermost-live-smoke-user@csit.local, and command-level login/status smoke reported loginComplete=true and statusConnected=true.
features.notes: Historical local-only bridge. Superseded by B04; command login no longer uses dev-login or bot-side pending chat login state.

features.title: Move login to AcornOps external integration account linking
features.user_visible_behavior: A Mattermost user can request login from @acorn-ops-bot, receive an AcornOps browser login link, complete OIDC auth, and have the bot recognize the authenticated AcornOps identity.

verification.command: ./init.sh plus local AcornOps login smoke
steps: Use only the Mattermost user id from event or websocket context.
Make @acorn-ops-bot login call POST /api/v1/auth/external-integrations/link in direct messages.
Keep password collection out of Mattermost.
Make status call POST /api/v1/auth/external-integrations/resolve.
Remove bot-side pending login/session storage and old transaction polling behavior.
Add automated tests and run ./init.sh.
Run a local AcornOps smoke test when the stack is available.

features.evidence: On 2026-06-04, reviewed the local AcornOps control-plane API under /Users/ryangoh/Desktop/Development/acornops/control-plane and recorded the route inventory in docs/acornops-api-inventory.md. Replaced the bot login command's dev-login path with an AcornOps OIDC browser login link generated from GET /api/v1/auth/oidc/login, added bot-side pending login state to src/bot/auth-store.js, and documented the session-storage decision in docs/bot-auth-sessions.md. Automated tests passed with npm test after the change. ./init.sh passed with 18 tests after artifact updates. Live AcornOps smoke against http://localhost:8081 passed after host-network approval: /health returned ok, auth config returned oidcEnabled=true with provider dex, OIDC login returned 302 Found to Dex, and command-level login/status smoke returned loginHasOidcLink=true and pendingStatus=true. On 2026-06-05, added CSIT-side support for a proposed AcornOps Mattermost chat-login transaction API: POST /api/v1/auth/chat/integration/login creates a pending backend transaction, GET /api/v1/auth/chat/integration/login/{id} refreshes completion, and status can complete a pending transaction into stored AcornOps user/session state. npm test passed with 23 tests and ./init.sh passed with 23 tests. Local AcornOps live smoke did not run on 2026-06-05 because http://localhost:8081/health was not listening. On 2026-06-05, added docs/acornops-chat-login-contract.md with complete AcornOps implementation instructions and updated harness pointers to make that the next action. On 2026-06-09, replaced the proposed transaction API with the current AcornOps account-link contract: login calls POST /api/v1/auth/chat/integration/link, status calls POST /api/v1/auth/chat/integration/resolve, the old src/bot/auth-store.js pending/session store was removed, and Mattermost identity is extracted from event context. npm test passed with 21 tests after the contract swap. ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and 21 tests. Live smoke did not run because AcornOps was not listening on localhost:8081 and Mattermost was not listening on localhost:8065. Later on 2026-06-09, user smoke showed direct-message events still missed required identity context, so the bot added trusted Mattermost context enrichment via config/client, channel lookup, exactly-one user team lookup, optional MATTERMOST_SERVER_ID / MATTERMOST_TEAM_ID defaults, and automatic .env loading. ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and 27 tests after the env-loader fix. On 2026-06-10, AcornOps changed the contract to require only mattermostUserId, scoped to a single Mattermost server. The bot was simplified to send only the post author's Mattermost user id for link and resolve requests, while keeping automatic .env loading. npm test passed with 24 tests and ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and 24 tests after the user-id-only update. The user then reported the updated Mattermost login and status flow works against the live stack. Current harness/docs cleanup removed stale live-smoke-next and proposed transaction-contract pointers. ./init.sh passed after the docs cleanup with harness verification, lint, build, and 24 tests. On 2026-06-17, AcornOps renamed the chat auth endpoint prefix to /auth/chat/integration/; CSIT updated the client, request-shape tests, and docs to use /api/v1/auth/chat/integration/link and /api/v1/auth/chat/integration/resolve. node --test test/acornops-client.test.js passed with 3 tests, and ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and 31 tests after the rename. On 2026-06-18, CSIT adopted the provider-neutral external integration request body and token naming: link and resolve now send { externalUserId } using only the observed Mattermost post author id, setup text uses EXTERNAL_INTEGRATION_SERVICE_TOKEN. Targeted tests passed with 33 tests, and ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and 41 tests. On 2026-06-23, CSIT updated account-link calls to POST /api/v1/auth/external-integrations/link and POST /api/v1/auth/external-integrations/resolve, required EXTERNAL_INTEGRATION_SERVICE_TOKEN, and added optional externalDisplayName on link creation from trusted Mattermost sender metadata. Targeted tests passed with 51 tests. The legacy MATTERMOST_CHAT_SERVICE_TOKEN fallback was then removed; targeted tests passed with 52 tests, and final ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and 65 tests. Live smoke for the new endpoint family did not run because local AcornOps and Mattermost were not listening.
features.notes: Depends on B03. Normal AcornOps user auth is cookie-session based; do not use Bearer tokens for browser user login. The bot must not store raw browser cookies, OIDC access tokens, ID tokens, refresh tokens, raw external integration link tokens, bot-side AcornOps user ids, or AcornOps browser sessions. AcornOps now owns durable external-integration-to-AcornOps links through /api/v1/auth/external-integrations/link and /api/v1/auth/external-integrations/resolve. Resolve sends only externalUserId; link creation sends externalUserId and may include optional externalDisplayName derived from trusted Mattermost metadata. The Mattermost adapter derives externalUserId from the observed post author id. On 2026-06-12, pre-B05 refactoring centralized bot runtime defaults, shared Mattermost/AcornOps JSON request plumbing, and message parsing helpers without changing account-link behavior.

features.title: Wire authenticated workspace command
features.user_visible_behavior: A linked Mattermost user can ask @acorn-ops-bot for workspaces and receive workspace summaries backed by AcornOps authorization.

verification.command: ./init.sh plus local AcornOps workspace-command smoke
steps: Call GET /api/v1/workspaces with the external integration service token.
Send x-acornops-external-user-id using the Mattermost post author's user id.
Keep login direct-message-only; later command policy is tracked by B07.
Return workspace names, plan, quota, empty-state, and pagination cursor details to the user.
Add automated tests for linked, unlinked, and backend-error responses.
Run ./init.sh.
Run a local AcornOps and Mattermost smoke test when the stack is available.

features.evidence: On 2026-06-18, added AcornOpsClient.listWorkspaces() for GET /api/v1/workspaces?limit=50 with Authorization Bearer EXTERNAL_INTEGRATION_SERVICE_TOKEN and x-acornops-external-user-id set from the observed Mattermost post author. Added /workspaces direct-message command handling, no-argument validation, workspace summary formatting, empty-state output, direct-message channel guard, 401 login guidance, and backend-error handling that does not echo response bodies. Targeted tests passed: node --test test/acornops-client.test.js with 4 tests, node --test test/bot-message.test.js with 19 tests, and node --test test/bot-runner.test.js with 7 tests. Final ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and 40 tests. Later on 2026-06-18, token naming moved to EXTERNAL_INTEGRATION_SERVICE_TOKEN and listWorkspaces uses the normalized externalUserId for x-acornops-external-user-id; ./init.sh passed with 41 tests after the external integration contract update. Live smoke did not run because Mattermost was not listening on localhost:8065 and AcornOps was not listening on localhost:8081.
features.notes: Depends on B04. Historical first workspace implementation was direct-message-only; B07 later allows authenticated read commands from channel mentions.

features.title: Wire authenticated workspace detail and cluster commands
features.user_visible_behavior: A linked Mattermost user can use numbered workspaces, inspect workspace details, set a current workspace, and list clusters backed by AcornOps authorization.

verification.command: ./init.sh plus local AcornOps cluster-command smoke
steps: Add GET /api/v1/workspaces/{workspaceId} support for workspace detail.
Add GET /api/v1/workspaces/{workspaceId}/kubernetes-clusters support for cluster listing.
Use the verified external integration identity to authorize workspace detail and cluster access.
Remember the last workspace list and current workspace per external user id inside the bot process.
Allow workspaces 1 to fetch detail without changing the current workspace.
Allow workspace 1 to fetch detail and update the current workspace.
Allow workspace to fetch details for the current workspace.
Allow clusters to use the current workspace.
Keep login direct-message-only; later command policy is tracked by B07.
Add automated tests for client request shape, context behavior, message behavior, and runner context reuse.
Run ./init.sh.
Run a local AcornOps and Mattermost smoke test when the stack is available.

features.evidence: On 2026-06-19, added AcornOpsClient.getWorkspace() for GET /api/v1/workspaces/{workspaceId} and AcornOpsClient.listKubernetesClusters() for GET /api/v1/workspaces/{workspaceId}/kubernetes-clusters?limit=50, both using Authorization Bearer EXTERNAL_INTEGRATION_SERVICE_TOKEN and x-acornops-external-user-id from the observed Mattermost post author. Added an in-memory per-external-user command context for the last workspace list and current workspace. /workspaces now returns numbered workspaces; /workspaces 1 fetches detail without setting current workspace; /workspace 1 fetches detail and sets the current workspace; /workspace shows details for the current workspace; /clusters uses the current workspace; /clusters 1 lists clusters for a remembered workspace index and selects it. Targeted tests passed: node --test test/acornops-client.test.js with 6 tests, node --test test/command-context.test.js with 3 tests, node --test test/bot-message.test.js with 24 tests, and node --test test/bot-runner.test.js with 8 tests. Final ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and 52 tests. On 2026-06-19, updated workspace detail behavior so /workspaces 1 is read-only and /workspace fetches full details for the current workspace. Targeted tests passed: node --test test/bot-message.test.js with 25 tests and node --test test/bot-runner.test.js with 8 tests. Final ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and 53 tests. Live smoke did not run because Mattermost was not listening on localhost:8065 and AcornOps was not listening on localhost:8081.
features.notes: Depends on B05. Historical cluster implementation used slash aliases and clusters 1 as a workspace selector; B07 later moves to plain commands and uses clusters 1 for cluster detail.

features.title: Wire expanded external integration read and assistant commands
features.user_visible_behavior: A linked Mattermost user can use plain bot commands from direct messages or channel mentions to inspect workspaces, clusters, VMs, resources, findings, investigations, and read-only assistant sessions.

verification.command: ./init.sh plus local AcornOps expanded-command smoke
steps: Reject slash-prefixed bot commands and accept plain commands only.
Keep login direct-message-only while allowing authenticated read and read-only assistant commands from channel mentions.
Add AcornOps client methods for cluster detail, resources, findings, investigations, VMs, sessions, messages, and read-only assistant message posting.
Track current workspace plus exactly one selected target, either cluster or VM, in process-local command context.
Clear target and session context when workspace changes; clear session context when cluster or VM changes.
Return a context header showing current workspace and selected target at the top of command responses.
Add automated tests for client request shape, command context behavior, message behavior, and runner behavior.
Run ./init.sh.
Run a local AcornOps and Mattermost smoke test when the stack is available.

features.evidence: On 2026-06-19, implemented the expanded external integration command surface from the updated endpoint contract. Commands are now accepted without a leading slash only, and only login remains direct-message-only. Added AcornOpsClient methods for GET /api/v1/workspaces/{workspaceId}/investigations, GET /api/v1/workspaces/{workspaceId}/kubernetes-clusters/{clusterId}, cluster resources/findings, VM list/detail/resources/findings, cluster and target sessions, session metadata/messages, POST /api/v1/sessions/{sessionId}/messages with toolAccessMode read_only, and GET /api/v1/runs/{runId}. Added cluster, resources, findings, investigations, vms, vm, sessions, session new, session, messages, and ask command handling. Expanded process-local command context for numbered clusters, VMs, sessions, current workspace, exactly one selected target, and current session. Focused verification passed with node --test test/acornops-client.test.js test/command-context.test.js test/bot-message.test.js reporting 45 passing tests. Final ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and 64 tests. Live smoke did not run because Mattermost and AcornOps local services were not available in this workspace.
features.notes: Depends on B06. Assistant run observation beyond returning the run id is not yet user-facing. The command context remains process-local and should move to shared TTL storage before multi-replica or restart-resilient deployments.

features.title: Redesign Mattermost bot UX around context and chat mode
features.user_visible_behavior: A linked Mattermost user can choose a workspace and target, start a read-only chat with AcornOps, ask follow-up questions as plain messages, pause/resume/end chat mode, and discover filters without a noisy main help page.

verification.command: ./init.sh plus local AcornOps chat-mode smoke
steps: Keep main help concise and focused on the common workflow.
Document filters through help filters and docs/wiki-mattermost-bot-commands.md.
Add generic targets and target selection as the primary target UX.
Keep clusters, vms, sessions, messages, and ask as compatibility commands outside the short help surface.
Add chat new, chat pause, chat resume, and chat end.
Treat non-command messages as read-only AcornOps questions while chat mode is active.
Track active/paused chat session, latest run, and one active streamed run in process-local context.
Follow long-running chat runs over SSE and post final answers back to Mattermost.
Run ./init.sh.
Run a local AcornOps and Mattermost smoke test when the stack is available.

features.evidence: On 2026-06-26, implemented the context-plus-chat UX redesign. Added AcornOpsClient.listTargets(), getTarget(), and listRunEvents(). Updated help to show the concise login -> workspaces -> workspace 1 -> targets -> target 1 -> chat new flow and added help filters with finite filter values. Added docs/wiki-mattermost-bot-commands.md for advanced filters, aliases, shortcuts, and compatibility assistant commands. Added generic targets/target commands. Added chat new, chat pause, chat resume, chat end, active chat-mode free-text question handling, latest-run context, and completed-run answer rendering when available. Kept sessions, session, messages, and ask as compatibility commands outside the short help surface. Focused verification passed with node --test test/command-context.test.js test/acornops-client.test.js test/bot-message.test.js reporting 52 passing tests. npm test passed with 72 tests. Final ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and 72 tests. Follow-up after live chat-mode testing changed chat questions to use hashed identifier-like clientMessageId values instead of raw externalUserId:hash strings, added a clearer rejected-chat-message response, and updated stale cluster/VM copy to reinforce targets and chat new. Follow-up verification passed with node --test test/bot-message.test.js reporting 38 passing tests and ./init.sh passing with harness verification, lint, build, and 72 tests. On 2026-06-29, live testing still returned HTTP 400 after chat new, so CSIT re-read the endpoint contract, confirmed the documented chat message body, added structured AcornOps error detail parsing, and changed chat-message 400 responses to surface safe AcornOps error code/message details rather than suggesting rephrasing. Focused verification passed with node --test test/bot-message.test.js reporting 39 passing tests, and ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and 73 tests. Later on 2026-06-29, CSIT changed chat questions to poll read-only runs briefly, return assistant replies directly, hide session/message/run ids by default, and use a non-technical still-working fallback. CSIT then made active chat mode modal: command-looking text such as status, resources, and findings is sent to the assistant until the user sends chat pause; chat controls remain available. Focused verification passed with node --test test/bot-message.test.js reporting 44 passing tests, and ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and 78 tests. Review follow-up on 2026-06-29 changed chat clientMessageId generation to use the Mattermost source post id when available, tightened assistant answer rendering to correlate with the accepted user message_id and exact assistant run id, and repointed local command-reference links to docs/wiki-mattermost-bot-commands.md. Focused verification passed with node --test test/bot-message.test.js test/bot-runner.test.js reporting 54 passing tests, and ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and 80 tests. Follow-up on 2026-06-30 simplified the message identity path so post.user_id is passed once as userId, message handling no longer accepts a compatibility identity parameter, and { externalUserId } is derived directly from userId; focused node --test test/bot-message.test.js test/bot-runner.test.js test/acornops-client.test.js passed with 61 tests, and ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and 79 tests. SSE follow-up work on 2026-06-30 added AcornOpsClient.streamRun() for GET /api/v1/runs/{runId}/stream, SSE parsing, process-local active streamed run state, and src/bot/run-follower.js for one active followed run per external user. The runner now starts follow-up delivery after posting the immediate acknowledgement; run_completed posts the final assistant answer, run_failed/run_cancelled post concise terminal messages, dropped streams reconnect and fall back to bounded polling, chat pause keeps following, and chat end aborts following and suppresses eventual results. Focused node --test test/acornops-client.test.js test/command-context.test.js test/bot-message.test.js test/bot-runner.test.js test/run-follower.test.js passed with 79 tests. Full npm test passed with 91 tests. Final ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and 91 tests. Review follow-up fixed generic target chat session routing so target 1 followed by chat new uses POST /api/v1/workspaces/{workspaceId}/targets/{targetId}/sessions instead of the compatibility Kubernetes cluster session endpoint. Focused node --test test/command-context.test.js test/bot-message.test.js test/bot-runner.test.js test/run-follower.test.js test/acornops-client.test.js passed with 80 tests. Full npm test passed with 92 tests. Final ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and 92 tests. Live smoke still needs to rerun for SSE follow-up delivery.
features.notes: Depends on B07. Chat mode remains process-local and read-only. V1 follows one active streamed run per external user. chat pause leaves an active run following in place; chat end aborts it. If the bot process restarts, active run follows are lost and AcornOps may complete without a Mattermost follow-up post.

features.title: Refactor bot command handling modules
features.user_visible_behavior: A developer can work on Mattermost command routing, formatting, argument parsing, error handling, and chat run polling in focused source modules instead of one oversized message handler file.

verification.command: ./init.sh
steps: Move pure command response formatting out of src/bot/message.js.
Move command argument parsing out of src/bot/message.js.
Move AcornOps command error copy out of src/bot/message.js.
Move chat run idempotency and polling helpers out of src/bot/message.js.
Keep existing command behavior and public message exports compatible.
Run npm test.
Run ./init.sh.

features.evidence: On 2026-06-30, split src/bot/message.js into focused modules under src/bot/commands/ and src/bot/chat/: commands/formatters.js, commands/args.js, commands/errors.js, commands/context.js, chat/runs.js, and chat/follower.js. The message dispatcher now imports these helpers instead of owning formatting, parsing, error translation, and chat run polling internals. Baseline ./init.sh passed before the refactor with harness verification, lint, build, and 92 tests. During the refactor, npm test passed with 92 tests after the formatter extraction, after the parser/error/chat-run extraction, and after moving the helpers into domain folders. Final ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and 92 tests.
features.notes: This is a structure-only refactor. Live Mattermost/AcornOps smoke was not rerun because no runtime behavior was intentionally changed. test/bot-message.test.js remains large and is a good future candidate for splitting by command area.

features.title: Dockerise bot runtime image
features.user_visible_behavior: A developer can build and verify a production-oriented AcornOps Mattermost bot Docker image without copying host node_modules or committed secrets.

verification.command: ./scripts/verify-docker.sh
steps: Rename active Mattermost runtime env vars to MATTERMOST_URL, MATTERMOST_BOT_TOKEN, and MATTERMOST_BOT_USERNAME.
Install dependencies inside Docker from package*.json instead of copying host node_modules.
Run bot lint, build, and tests in a Docker verify target.
Build the final runtime image as acornops-mattermost-bot:local.
Provide a bot-only docker-compose.yml with host-local Mattermost and AcornOps URL defaults.
Keep secrets out of the image build context.
Run npm test and ./scripts/verify-docker.sh.

features.evidence: On 2026-07-01, renamed active Mattermost runtime env vars to MATTERMOST_URL, MATTERMOST_BOT_TOKEN, and MATTERMOST_BOT_USERNAME, and renamed active chat timing knobs to CHAT_RUN_POLL_ATTEMPTS, CHAT_RUN_POLL_INTERVAL_MS, RUN_STREAM_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS, RUN_STREAM_RECONNECT_DELAY_MS, RUN_STREAM_FALLBACK_POLL_INTERVAL_MS, and RUN_STREAM_FALLBACK_POLL_MAX_MS. Added Dockerfile, .dockerignore, docker-compose.yml, and scripts/verify-docker.sh. Baseline ./init.sh passed before changes with harness verification, lint, build, and 92 tests. npm test passed after changes with 93 tests. Initial Docker verification exposed that test/env.test.js used macOS-specific /private/tmp; the test now uses os.tmpdir(). Final npm test passed with 93 tests. docker-compose.yml provides host-local defaults for MATTERMOST_URL and ACORNOPS_API_BASE_URL through host.docker.internal. Final ./scripts/verify-docker.sh passed after building the verify target, running npm run verify:bot inside node:22-bookworm-slim with 93 tests, and building acornops-mattermost-bot:local.
features.notes: Full image publishing, environment templates, Compose, and Kubernetes orchestration remain in acornops-deployment. The bot should run as a single active replica until command context and SSE run-following state move to shared TTL storage. docker-compose.yml is a local developer convenience only; production Compose/Kubernetes orchestration still belongs in acornops-deployment.

features.evidence: On 2026-07-03, changed command parsing so normal bot commands require a ! prefix on the command word only, while arguments remain unchanged. Slash-prefixed input now guides users to use !, and unprefixed main-conversation messages nudge users toward !help. Registered chat-thread replies are routed as assistant questions without requiring !. Added parser, message, runner, and docs coverage. Baseline ./init.sh passed before changes with 93 tests. Final ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and 104 tests.
features.notes: This is the first step in the threaded multi-chat and webhook UX roadmap.

Route replies by Mattermost channel_id and root_id to the matching AcornOps session.

features.evidence: On 2026-07-03, added Postgres-backed command context behind BOT_DATABASE_URL with in-memory fallback, schema migrations, user context persistence, chat thread mappings, active run records, user-level webhook routes, and inbound event idempotency. Reworked !chat new to create an AcornOps session plus a Mattermost thread root, route replies by channel_id and root_id, post assistant replies and SSE follow-ups into the correct thread, restrict first-version participation to the creating Mattermost user, and make !chat end close only the current thread. Baseline ./init.sh passed before changes with 93 tests. Final ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and 104 tests.
features.notes: Thread participation is restricted to the Mattermost user who created the chat in the first version.

features.title: Add workflow listing, launching, and threaded runs
features.user_visible_behavior: Users can list eligible active read-only, read-write, and approval-gated AcornOps workflows, launch one with validated inputs and Workflow V2 resource references, follow its streamed result in a dedicated Mattermost thread, and continue the same workflow session by replying in that thread.

Route plain thread replies to the same workflow session and allow one active run per thread.
Persist workflow thread kind, workflow identity, launch inputs, and active run state in memory and Postgres.
Preserve the external integration workflow safety restrictions.
Add automated tests and run ./init.sh.

features.evidence: Implemented on 2026-07-09. Added !workflows and !workflow run <number|id> [key=value...] with quoted string input parsing, exact context-grant derivation, selected target binding, workflow session creation, and launch messages. Added dedicated **Workflow launched: <name>** Mattermost roots, persisted workflow thread context, same-session plain-text replies, one active run per thread, shared !chat end closure, and workflow-aware SSE terminal output. Focused node --test test/workflows.test.js test/acornops-client.test.js test/command-context.test.js test/postgres-store.test.js test/bot-message.test.js test/bot-runner.test.js test/run-follower.test.js passed with 104 tests. A manual smoke follow-up confirmed list and session creation succeed, while AcornOps rejected the run with AI_PROVIDER_CREDENTIAL_MISSING; the bot now surfaces that safe 400 code/message. Final ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and 131 tests.
features.notes: Workflow launch idempotency uses the required clientRequestId derived from the originating Mattermost post and a persisted source-post session reservation. Required non-target prompt-resource placeholders still need to be filled in the AcornOps console. Automatic follower recovery after restart remains separate work.

Keep signature verification, route lookup, and duplicate suppression unchanged.

features.evidence: Implemented on 2026-07-10. Local AcornOps docs/source confirmed lastSeenAt is the last observed issue evidence timestamp, while resolvedAt is set when an issue becomes resolved. Updated route webhook alert formatting in src/bot/server.js to render rich issue lifecycle alerts for issue.created.v1, issue.reopened.v1, and issue.resolved.v1; generic non-issue deliveries now render as AcornOps info alerts using occurredAt, createdAt, timestamp, then webhook received time. Final ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and 133 tests. Focused node --test test/config.test.js test/bot-server.test.js passed with 19 tests. npm run verify:bot passed with lint, build, and 133 tests.

features.title: Validate login-triggered context against AcornOps account changes
features.user_visible_behavior: When a Mattermost user relinks after expiry, the bot preserves context for the same AcornOps account but resets stale workspace, target, and thread context if the relink completes against a different AcornOps account.

verification.command: ./init.sh plus live account-switch smoke when services are available
steps: Store a hashed AcornOps account fingerprint and login-validation-pending flag in command context.
Make !login resolve the current link before creating a link URL.
Preserve context while an unlinked or expired user completes a new login URL.
Resolve once on the next authenticated command after !login and reset context only when the account fingerprint changes.
Make !login reset explicitly clear command context before returning a new login URL.
Reject stale Mattermost action buttons after a context reset.
Leave webhook routes untouched.
Add automated tests and run ./init.sh.

features.evidence: Implemented on 2026-07-10. Added accountFingerprint, loginValidationPending, and contextGeneration to command context; resetAccountContext clears workspace/target/session/list/run state and chat/workflow thread mappings while leaving webhook routes intact. !login now resolves first, returns an already-linked message for linked users, creates a link URL and marks validation pending for unlinked users, and supports !login reset. The next authenticated command after a pending login resolves once, preserves context for the same AcornOps user fingerprint, or resets context before command execution if the fingerprint changed. Mattermost workspace/target button payloads include contextGeneration and stale callbacks are rejected after resets. Focused node --test test/bot-message.test.js test/bot-runner.test.js test/bot-server.test.js test/command-context.test.js test/postgres-store.test.js test/run-follower.test.js passed with 107 tests. npm run lint, npm run build, npm test with 139 tests, and final ./init.sh with 139 tests passed.
features.notes: The bot stores a hash of AcornOps user.id, not the raw AcornOps user id. Live smoke should cover same-account relogin after expiry and different-account relink. Webhook route cleanup remains out of scope for this feature.

Keep approval decisions unavailable to the bot.

features.evidence: Implemented on 2026-07-10. Added explicit !chat new --write [title] with a fresh GET workspace effective-permission preflight for create_read_write_runs. Persisted toolAccessMode and workspaceId per Mattermost thread in memory and Postgres, passed read_write on every message in enabled threads, and kept read_only as the default. Added ACORNOPS_CONSOLE_BASE_URL and SSE handling for tool_approval_requested, approved, rejected, and expired events; approval requests link to /workspaces/{workspaceId}/approvals?runId={runId}&approvalId={approvalId}, resolution events do not end the follower, and final output remains in the same thread. Focused chat/follower tests passed with 75 tests. Final ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and 144 tests.

features.user_visible_behavior: Created and reopened issue alerts offer a Run Triage button that links to recent AcornOps cluster chat activity or starts a new read-only triage run in a Mattermost thread.

features.evidence: Implemented on 2026-07-13. Created/reopened issue alerts now include an acting-user-bound Run Triage action. The callback reloads the issue through GET /api/v1/workspaces/{workspaceId}/targets/{targetId}/issues, loads cluster metadata, and checks GET /api/v1/workspaces/{workspaceId}/targets/{targetId}/chat-activity. Recent activity returns an AcornOps deep link; otherwise the bot creates a cluster session, posts the exact console triage prompt with read_only access, registers a Mattermost thread, and starts the shared SSE follower. Focused AcornOps client/server tests passed with 31 tests. Final ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and 148 tests. On 2026-07-15, an iterative review updated the repository command reference and published GitHub wiki commit 366f26a with the Run Triage behavior; focused command/server verification passed with 86 tests and final ./init.sh passed with all 148 tests.

Production requires BOT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL, MATTERMOST_ACTION_SECRET, and ACORNOPS_CONSOLE_BASE_URL. Live signed webhook/button rendering, both triage action paths, and end-to-end assistant streaming still need smoke testing.

features.title: Align workflows, approvals, issues, and response UX with the external integration contract
features.user_visible_behavior: Users can list workspace issues, launch every eligible AcornOps workflow, follow multi-step executions, and confirm bot-originated approval decisions directly in Mattermost with clearer command responses.

steps: Replace findings and investigations with one workspace-wide !issues command and current issue filters.

Follow aggregate replayable workflow execution streams through every step.
Render user-bound Approve and Reject actions and require a confirmed Mattermost dialog before submitting a decision.

Update command and runtime documentation.
Add automated tests and run ./init.sh.

features.evidence: Implemented and verified on 2026-07-18. Added workspace-wide !issues with exact contract filters and generic unknown responses for removed !findings/!investigations. Eligible workflow modes now use stable Mattermost-post-derived clientRequestId values, consume executionId, and follow replayable aggregate streams through multiple attempts with cursor persistence and deduplication. Troubleshooting and workflow approval requests now offer user-bound Approve/Reject confirmation dialogs, signed immutable state, run-scoped decisions, settled-state handling, and button removal, with console fallback when callbacks are unavailable. The earlier target-reference fix rendered the selected target mention; its corrected live request returned HTTP 202. Focused client/command/runner/follower/context/action tests passed with 148 tests. Full npm test passed with 160 tests; lint and build passed; final ./init.sh passed with harness verification and all 160 tests. Mattermost and AcornOps were not listening on localhost:8065/8081, so the full live smoke matrix remains outstanding. On 2026-07-19, the post-implementation review was fully remediated: all action types moved to expiring signed tokens with a server-only key; approval dialog state became purpose-bound and short-lived; initial workflow post/session reservations became durable and single-process concurrency-safe; workflow cursors and dedupe state now commit after Mattermost delivery; non-terminal workflows retain active state across repeated reconnect/poll cycles; and AcornOps decision success is separated from retryable Mattermost post updates. Focused regression verification passed with 119 tests, full npm test passed with 170 tests, and final ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and all 170 tests. A final review of all 18 unpushed commits fixed retry loss after failed Mattermost webhook posting and bound Run Triage tokens to the alert channel. Focused regression verification passed with 37 tests and final ./init.sh passed with all 172 tests. On 2026-07-23, strict message-shape hardening removed unsupported launch fields so workflow messages contain exactly content and clientRequestId. The production-readiness sweep then corrected the remaining Workflow V2 drift: the bot now consumes capabilityPolicy, resourceRequirements, prompt, and @target references, renders declared inputs into content, and persists launch-target metadata for stable follow-ups; session creation remains authoritative only for workspace, pinned definition, and approved context grants. Final ./init.sh passed with harness verification, lint, build, and all 175 tests.
features.notes: Generated workflow report delivery, multi-replica launch serialization, and automatic active-follower recovery after a bot restart remain separate follow-up work. Existing buttons using the former raw-context shape become invalid after deployment. Live Mattermost/AcornOps approval, multi-step workflow, prolonged-disconnect, and issue smoke is still required when services are available.

features.title: Add a seeded task-managed local Mattermost bot stack
features.user_visible_behavior: A developer can start, inspect, smoke-test, stop, preserve, or fully reset a complete local Mattermost bot environment without manually creating the test user, team, channel, bot account, memberships, or bot token.

verification.command: task validate plus task local-up, task local-smoke, task local-down, and task local-reset lifecycle verification
steps: Provide deployment-style Task commands for prerequisites, startup, seeding, smoke, status, logs, shutdown, and reset.
Start Mattermost and bot Postgres services plus Mattermost and the bot with explicit health dependencies.
Build Mattermost natively for Docker amd64 or arm64 from a checksum-verified official Team Edition archive.
Idempotently seed a local administrator, team, channel, bot account, memberships, and ignored bot token by default.
Verify health, seeded entities, and a real mentioned bot command/reply round trip.
Prove local-down preserves state and local-reset cleanly recreates it.
Document credentials, ports, ownership boundaries, flags, and secret handling.

features.evidence: Implemented and live-verified on 2026-07-23. Mattermost 11.7.0 built and ran natively on macOS arm64 from the official Team Edition archive with published SHA-256 verification. First seed created the dev administrator, csit-lab team, chatops-lab channel, acorn-ops-bot account, memberships, and an ignored mode-0600 token; a second seed reused all entities and the token. local-down/local-up preserved state. local-reset removed only the project volumes and generated token, after which local-up recreated and reseeded the stack. task local-smoke passed Mattermost, bot, and AcornOps health, seeded-entity checks, and a real !help command/reply WebSocket round trip. Final task validate passed Compose validation, lint, build, and all 177 tests.
features.notes: The stack intentionally leaves AcornOps platform lifecycle and data to acornops-deployment. SEED_MATTERMOST_DATA=false supports manual token setups, and CHECK_ACORNOPS=false supports intentionally isolated bot smoke. The generated bot token is never printed or committed.
